name: ETH 4h Inventory Reset Reclaim v1
edge_mechanism: price flushes through short-term value, weak inventory is cleared, value is reclaimed, and price rotates away from the failed flush
thesis: ETH 4h remains the cleanest pure-chop lane in the basket, so if there is still a place for reclaim logic it has to be a denser inventory-reset reacceptance, not the sparse staged-recovery family that just died on count. The edge is the reclaim after the flush, not catching the knife and not asking for rare continuation persistence.
invalidation_condition: If ETH 4h flush-and-reclaim events still undertrade or fail to rotate enough after reclaim, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price flushes below short-term value, weak inventory is cleared, value is reclaimed, and price rotates back toward balance.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In pure chop, failed inventory flushes should recur more often than durable directional continuations. This is denser and more mechanism-faithful than the previous staged recovery concept.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is the best fit because it is still the cleanest pure-chop lane and should support value-reclaim rotation better than DOGE transition or TAO trend continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The reclaim may only be a weak bounce inside unresolved directional pressure, causing repeated failure before first target.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If density is acceptable but expectancy is thin, move profit-taking closer before changing the reclaim trigger.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Repeated modest balance rotations, many first-target wins, and only occasional larger unwinds.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 4h clears screen density and produces materially better trade count than the prior sparse value-recovery family.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-15 trades, or repeated reclaims that fail immediately back under value.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because failed flushes in chop should recur

test_lanes.why_included: Best-fit pure-chop lane for inventory-reset reclaim rotation.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH 4h shows enough reset-reclaim rotations with positive first-target expectancy.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h still undertrades or the moves remain too shallow after costs.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Moderate count with many balance rotations.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same reclaim grammar survives on the transition-heavy primary lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: DOGE 4h retains a tradable reset-reclaim behavior.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: DOGE 4h confirms the idea is too chop-specific.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Slightly larger but less frequent reclaim rotations than ETH.

test_lanes.why_included: Useful control to see whether the reclaim logic is too anti-trend on a trend-capable lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: TAO 4h still shows failed flushes snapping back to value cleanly.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h confirms the idea should remain chop-owned.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Lower count, stronger but rarer reclaim rotations than ETH.

long: Exit remaining 20% on loss of VWAP or 5 bars
short: Exit remaining 20% on reclaim of VWAP or 5 bars
